query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "agentic abstention: do agents know when to stop instead of act?" computer science
rationale: This paper satisfies acceptance criteria (a) and (b) as it explicitly investigates the mechanism of "agentic abstention" and "uncertainty-aware decision making" in AI agents, which is the core subject of the user's research question, even though it applies this mechanism to a specific medical imaging domain rather than the general computer science context of the original "llmXive" work.
